description: A trap-rigged store-room a Circus runner would recognise on sight. Spot the tripwires, disarm what you can, and turn the room's own tricks back on the guard who comes to investigate.
customIntroduction: The door gives to a thin blade and a steadier nerve. Inside, the air is close and oiled — a smuggler's store-room, and a careful one. Wren has run rooms like this before, in another life, for another paymaster. The first rule the Circus teaches: the floor is never just a floor. Somewhere ahead is the cache. Somewhere closer is a tripwire. And a guard is due to make his round.
customContext: TRAP / STEALTH / GEAR TUTORIAL for Wren Allowyn (Halfling Rogue). Teaches three mechanics: (1) DETECTING + DISARMING traps — two concealed traps are rigged in the room; Wren spots them with passive Perception or the SEARCH action and removes them with DISARM (Dexterity / Sleight of Hand, advantage with her Thieves' Tools). (2) AREA-DENIAL GEAR — Wren can deploy Caltrops or Ball Bearings (SET buttons) to create a hazard zone the guard must cross. (3) STEALTH + AMBUSH — Wren can HIDE and open with a Sneak Attack. One bandit guard patrols. Reward clever use of the room over a straight fight. Keep it light and tense, not deadly.
objective: Disarm the rigged traps, deal with the guard, and reach the cache. Use Wren's gear — caltrops, ball bearings, and her Thieves' Tools — to turn the room against him.

traps.name: Tripwire Snare
traps.triggeredMessage: A hidden wire snaps taut and a noose of cord whips around your ankle!

traps.name: Dart Trap
traps.triggeredMessage: A pressure plate clicks — darts hiss from a seam in the wall!

then.message: Open on Wren stepping into the smuggler's store-room. Set the close, oiled air and the runner's instinct that the floor is rigged. Remind the player, in the fiction, that she carries Thieves' Tools and area-denial gear (caltrops, ball bearings) and that a guard is due. Invite her to move carefully — SEARCH for traps, HIDE, or set a hazard before the guard arrives.
